prose:
Computed property: Total electronic energy (E_total) = –215.08 eV.
Method provenance:
• Property: Single-point total electronic energy (electronic), units eV.
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical extended tight-binding; no Gaussian basis set).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied coordinates (file “oxygen.xyz”); single-point calculation — geometry not re-optimized (GFN2-xTB//input geometry).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge & multiplicity: charge 0 (neutral), multiplicity 3 → open-shell triplet treated with unrestricted formalism.
• Software / version: xTB  (via chemkit single-point-energy skill; see CLI in result JSON). Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF self-consistent; engine integrity check status “ok” (trustworthy = true). No warnings reported.

The calculation was launched through chemkit; its live log was written to the working directory (see path in the `cli_invocation` field of the result JSON) and contains the full PySCF/xtb stdout. The energy zero for GFN2-xTB is defined relative to isolated atoms at infinite separation and is not directly comparable to ab-initio total energies.

Result: E_total(GFN2-xTB, gas-phase O₂, triplet) = –215.08 eV.
